votes.reason: The Python code correctly translates the Coq logic, including the digit counting and the fizz_buzz summation over the correct range.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, including the digit counting helper and the main loop over the range up to n.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq implementation, including the digit counting logic with fuel and the fizz buzz loop over the correct range.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code. count_digit_7_aux iterates through digits counting 7s with the same fuel-based termination. count_digit_7 passes k as fuel (sufficient for any positive k). fizz_buzz_impl sums count_digit_7 for all i in [0,n) where i is divisible by 11 or 13. The pre and spec functions match the Coq definitions exactly in behavior.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it iterates 0..n-1, checks divisibility by 11 or 13, and sums digit-7 counts using the same mod/div rules with early stop when k==0. Hence results match for all natural n.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it counts 7-digits by iterating through decimal digits with a fuel cap and sums over i in [0,n) where i is divisible by 11 or 13. Edge cases like n=0 and i=0 match. For nonnegative inputs, results coincide.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it counts 7s by iterating digits until k becomes 0 with fuel=k, and sums this for i in [0,n) divisible by 11 or 13. Pre and spec match the Coq definitions for all natural-number inputs.
